name: Semantic Zoom Intent
summary: Representational (level-of-detail) zoom — a zoom where each level is a DIFFERENT REPRESENTATION of the same data, not a scaled view of one representation (a chip → a card-with-bar → an expanded panel-with-checklist). The level axis is the intent's own dimension: which representation renders is chosen by the current LOD level. Genuinely distinct from geometric `viewport-transform` (pan/zoom of one representation, which disclaims representation), `hierarchy` (tree traversal/drill-down), and `density` (detail-tier of one layout). A coined, studied, shipped pattern: Microsoft's named `SemanticZoom` control (grouped-header view ↔ item view), Pad++ (Bederson & Hollan, UIST'94, which coined 'semantic zooming'), DeepZoom/Seadragon, Maps LOD (labels → cities → streets → footprints), Photos Years→Months→Days→All, Calendar Day/Week/Month/Year, and Shneiderman's mantra 'overview first, zoom and filter, then details-on-demand' (1996). Minted from #2533 Fork 3.

trigger.description: What advances the level of detail. `zoom` (default) is a pinch / Ctrl+scroll / zoom control — the Microsoft `SemanticZoom` and Maps model. `viewport` selects the level automatically from available space — the responsive / text-greeking case (Figma renders placeholder text when far, full detail when near). `explicit` is a discrete view switch — Calendar's Day/Week/Month/Year buttons, Photos' Years/Days control.

transition.description: How one representation gives way to the next. `snap` (default) is a discrete swap at a threshold — the Microsoft `SemanticZoom` grouped↔item swap, Photos' Years/Months/Days tiers. `crossfade` blends the two representations across the threshold — the DeepZoom/Seadragon and Maps tile morph. The swap itself is representational either way; `crossfade` only softens the visual handoff.

levels.description: The shape of the level set. `binary` is two representations — the Microsoft `SemanticZoom` grouped-header view ↔ individual-item view. `graded` (default) is an ordered ladder of three or more tiers — Maps (labels → cities → streets → footprints → indoor plans), Photos (Years → Months → Days → All), Calendar (Day → Week → Month → Year). Each level names a distinct representation, not a scale factor.

description:
What This Is

Representational (level-of-detail) zoom — a zoom where each level is a different representation of the same data, not a scaled view of one representation. The board's chip → card-with-bar → expanded-panel-with-checklist is one item at three LOD levels; Maps' labels → cities → streets → building footprints is one place at five. The level axis is this intent's own dimension: which representation renders is chosen by the current level. Minted from #2533 Fork 3.

A coined, studied, shipped pattern

This is not a novel coinage — a platform vendor shipped it as a named control: Microsoft's SemanticZoom (Windows 8 / UWP) switches, on a pinch or Ctrl+scroll, between a zoomed-out view of grouped headers and a zoomed-in view of individual items — two different representations of one data source. Bederson & Hollan's Pad++ (UIST '94) coined "semantic zooming"; DeepZoom/Seadragon and Maps LOD render different content per level; Apple/Google Photos (Years → Months → Days → All) and Calendar (Day/Week/Month/Year) are distinct representations along a zoom axis, not four scalings. It instantiates Shneiderman's Visual Information-Seeking Mantra — "overview first, zoom and filter, then details-on-demand" (1996).

Distinct from geometric zoom, traversal, and density

Three nearby intents own adjacent turf; semantic zoom is none of them:

  • Viewport Transform — geometric pan/zoom of one representation (scale + translate a surface). It explicitly disclaims representation. Do NOT extend it: conflating a scale factor with a representation swap is the exact mistake this intent avoids. (The two often pair — Maps do both — but they are separate contracts.)
  • Hierarchy — tree traversal / drill-down (parent → child navigation). Semantic zoom re-represents the same node; it does not move to a different node.
  • Density — the detail-tier (spacing/touch-target) of one layout. Density tunes how tightly a single representation packs; semantic zoom swaps the representation itself.

Fixed mechanic — the focused datum survives the swap

Every conforming surface keeps the focused item anchored across a level change: the datum under focus (or the pinch centroid) stays put as its representation is replaced, so a user never loses their place when the view re-renders — the Microsoft SemanticZoom "the item you pinched stays in view" contract. Keyboard parity is non-negotiable: a zoom control (or +/- / an explicit level control) advances the level, and each levelchange is announced to assistive tech. A gesture-only surface is non-conforming.

Dimensions

  • trigger: zoom (default, pinch/Ctrl+scroll) · viewport (auto by space — greeking) · explicit (a discrete view control).
  • transition: snap (default, discrete swap at a threshold) · crossfade (blended morph).
  • levels: binary (two representations, MS SemanticZoom) · graded (default, an ordered 3+ ladder — Maps/Photos/Calendar).

Composition

Composes Viewport Transform when a surface also pans/zooms geometrically (the two run on one gesture, as Maps do), Gesture for the pinch/Ctrl+scroll recognition, and Density within a single level's layout. The level → representation binding is the app's; this intent owns the level axis and the swap contract. WE owns the intent; FUI ships the rendered LOD surface.

Interface Protocol

type LODTrigger = 'zoom' | 'viewport' | 'explicit';type LODTransition = 'snap' | 'crossfade';type LODLevels = 'binary' | 'graded';interface SemanticZoomIntent {    // what advances the level of detail    trigger: LODTrigger;    // how one representation gives way to the next    transition: LODTransition;    // the shape of the level set (two views, or an ordered ladder)    levels: LODLevels;    // the current level index into the ordered representation ladder    level: number;    // the ordered representations; representation[level] renders now    representations: readonly unknown[];}
